author: Ari Koslow
title: Randy Arozarena (hamstring) considered day-to-day
desc: The Mariners say Randy Arozarena is day-to-day.
impact: Arozarena was removed from Friday's game early after attempting to beat out a ground ball. He told reporters that his left hamstring tightened up. "Just a couple days and should be good," he said.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Trent Grisham placed on 10-day injured list
desc: Trent Grisham has been placed on the 10-day injured list with a right hamstring strain by the New York Yankees the club announced.
impact: Grisham joins sluggers Giancarlo Stanton and Aaron Judge on the injured list, leaving manager Aaron Boone scrambling to fill his lineup card with reliable options currently. In a corresponding move, Jasson Dominguez has been reinstated from the injured list and will take Grisham's place on the roster.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Juan Soto goes deep in win over Braves
desc: Juan Soto went 2-for-3 in a win over the Braves with a solo home run.
impact: Soto has provided mixed results at the plate of late, collecting hits in five of his last 10 games. The outfielder remains one of the few explosive bats as part of an underperforming Mets lineup, carrying a .931 OPS with 15 home runs, 32 RBI, 31 runs scored, and six stolen bases across 52 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Bo Bichette's two-homer, six-RBI outburst fuels Mets win
desc: Bo Bichette went 2-for-3 in a 7-5 win over the Braves with two home runs, two runs scored, and six RBI.
impact: Bichette has heated up at the plate of late, registering hits in six of his last eight contests. The infielder remains one of many inconsistent options in the Mets lineup for manager Carlos Mendoza, slashing .232 with eight home runs, 40 RBI, 33 runs scored, and one stolen base across 69 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Devin Williams locks down save in win over Braves
desc: Devin Williams struck out two batters while allowing two hits over 1 1/3 scoreless innings to earn the save over the Braves.
impact: Williams continues a mostly steady stretch out of the bullpen, holding opponents scoreless in five of his last six trips to the mound. The right-hander remains filled with risk despite holding onto his role as the primary closing options in the Mets bullpen for manager Carlos Mendoza, posting a 5.01 ERA and 1.50 WHIP with 10 saves through 26 appearances.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Nolan McLean battles control in no-decision
desc: Nolan McLean allowed two earned runs on three hits and four walks while striking out six over four innings in Friday's win over the Braves.
impact: McLean has fought control issues on the mound of late, walking three or more batters in each of his last three starts. The right-hander continues to fight consistency issues as part of frustrating Mets rotation for manager Carlos Mendoza, carrying a 4.01 ERA and 1.14 WHIP with a 3-4 record and 10.4 K/9 ratio across 14 starts.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Willson Contreras clobbers Rangers in 10-1 rout
desc: Willson Contreras went 3-for-4 in Friday's win over the Rangers with a home run, a double, two runs scored, two RBI, and one walk.
impact: Contreras has provided mixed results at the plate of late, collecting hits in five of his last nine contests. The 34-year-old offers manager Chad Tracy one of the more explosive bats as part of an inconsistent Red Sox lineup, carrying a .926 OPS with 14 home runs, 41 RBI, 31 runs scored, and one stolen base across 65 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Wilyer Abreu racks up three extra-base hits in win over Rangers
desc: Wilyer Abreu went 3-for-4 in a 10-1 win over the Rangers with two doubles, one home run, three runs scored, and three RBI.
impact: Abreu has heated back up at the plate, registering hits in eight of his last 11 contests. The 26-year-old remains one of the more stable options as part of an inconsistent Red Sox lineup, slashing .280 with eight homers, 32 RBI, 31 runs scored, and four steals through 66 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Ceddanne Rafaela torches Rangers with three extra-base hits
desc: Ceddanne Rafaela went 3-for-5 in a 10-1 win over the Rangers with a home run, two doubles, three runs, scored, three RBI, and one stolen base.
impact: Rafaela has caught fire at the plate, picking up hits in seven of his last nine contests. The 25-year-old remains one of the more impactful options as part of an underperforming Red Sox lineup, slashing .287 with seven home runs, 30 RBI, 29 runs scored, and seven stolen bases across 64 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Sonny Gray cruises as Red Sox steamroll Rangers
desc: Sonny Gray allowed one earned run on five hits while striking out seven over six innings in Friday's 10-1 win over the Rangers.
impact: Gray continues a solid stretch on the mound, allowing one earned run or less in five of his last seven starts. The right-hander continues to offer manager Chad Tracy one of the more steady arms in the Red Sox rotation, posting a 3.03 ERA and 1.20 WHIP with an 8-1 record and 7.4 K/9 ratio across 12 starts.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Jack Leiter shelled on mound in loss to Red Sox
desc: Jack Leiter allowed five earned runs on eight hits and two walks while striking out three over five innings in Friday's loss to the Red Sox.
impact: Leiter has struggled to miss opposing bats, allowing four earned runs or more in four of his last five starts. The right-hander remains one of the more underwhelming starting options in the Rangers rotation, posting a 4.97 ERA and 1.41 WHIP with a 3-6 record and 9.4 K/9 ratio across 14 starts.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Javier Assad cruises on mound in win over Giants
desc: Javier Assad allowed three hits and one walk while striking out five over six scoreless innings in Friday's win over the Giants.
impact: Assad has settled down on the mound of late, allowing one earned run or less in each of his last five trips to the mound. The right-hander has offered manager Craig Counsell another inconsistent arm in the Cubs staff, posting a 3.99 ERA and 1.02 WHIP to go with a 4-1 record and 2.7 K/BB ratio across 10 appearances.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Michael Busch cashes in with three-run blast in win over Giants
desc: Michael Busch went 1-for-3 in a 5-1 win over the Giants with a three-run home run and one walk.
impact: Busch continues a torrid stretch at the plate, collecting hits in 16 of his last 18 hits. The 28-year-old remains one of many streaky options in the Cubs lineup for manager Craig Counsell, slashing .252 with eight home runs, 41 RBI, 30 runs scored, and one stolen base across 69 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Bryce Eldridge goes deep in loss to Cubs
desc: Bryce Eldridge went 3-for-4 in Friday's 5-1 loss to the Cubs with a solo home run.
impact: Eldridge continues an impactful stretch of late, extending his on-base streak to 18 contests in Friday night's loss. The 21-year-old has rewarded manager Tony Vitello with one of the more explosive bats as part of a streaky Giants lineup, posting a .959 OPS with five home runs, 13 RBI, and 18 runs scored through 29 games.

author: Chris Schommer
title: Landen Roupp labors in shortened outing in loss to Cubs
desc: Landen Roupp allowed four earned runs on four hits and two walks while striking out five over 4 2/3 innings in Friday's 5-1 loss to the Cubs.
impact: Roupp has struggled to miss opposing bats of late, allowing four or more runs in five of his last eight trips to the mound. The right-hander has turned into a risky starting option while fighting inconsistent command as part of an underperforming Giants rotation, posting a 4.24 ERA and 1.29 WHIP with a 5-7 record and 9.9 K/9 ratio across 14 starts.
